comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review (critic-agent)

**Note on tooling:** The issue-creation endpoint `POST /api/issues/rm-09300` and the
list endpoint `GET /api/issues/rm-09300?status=...` both return `invalid problem id`
on this backend, even though issue `rm-09300-1` exists and accepts comments/docs.
I therefore cannot file the four gaps as separate `proof-gap` issues; they are
recorded below (each as a self-contained TITLE + BODY) and in the attached
"Critic Analysis" doc.

No prior `docs_*.md`/`docs_index.txt` files exist in the workspace, so this is a
from-scratch review (only `problem.tex`, `preamble.tex`, `solution.tex`, `SKILL.md`
are present).

---

This is a philosophy-of-science problem; the solution is a clean reconstruction of
Popper's instrumentalism critique (applied-as-instrument vs. pure-as-conjecture;
predictions as potential falsifiers). Its **formal/deductive packaging is valid**,
and §5 (predictions = potential falsifiers / tests / bearers of empirical content)
correctly answers the sub-question. The weaknesses are all at the level of
premise-justification and failure to engage the strongest opposing view. Four
genuine gaps.

### GAP 1 — Lemma 1 begs the question against constructive empiricism (P0).
Lemma 1 ("instruments are not true or false") is proved purely from Definition 1(A),
which was *written* so instrumental appraisal is "ineliminably indexed to that end."
Since the entire conclusion (Prop. 1, Thm. 1) flows from Lemma 1, the key premise is
a disguised restatement of the thesis. Van Fraassen's constructive empiricism
appraises theories by EMPIRICAL ADEQUACY = truth of observable consequences — a
NON-relational truth predicate restricted to observables. So the dichotomy
"purpose-indexed convenience vs. non-relational truth" is a false dichotomy that
excludes the strongest instrumentalist by fiat. FIX: argue independently (not by
definition) that no defensible instrumentalism can confine truth-aptness to
observable consequences, or re-target the thesis to capture empirical adequacy and
refute that.

### GAP 2 — Lemma 5 / Lemma 3 ignore the Duhem–Quine thesis (P0).
By confirmation holism, an observed clash refutes only `T ∧ auxiliaries ∧ initial
conditions ∧ observational background`, never an isolated universal law. So Lemma 5's
"a single counter-instance establishes the conjecture is false" is logically false as
stated. Worse for the needed CONTRAST: "restrict the range / blame an auxiliary /
retain the theory" is not the exclusive signature of instrumental use — realists do
it constantly (Lakatos's protective belt; Kuhnian normal science). The asymmetry of
Prop. 2 therefore collapses, and the claim that localization "just is" switching from
(C) to (A) is an unargued stipulation contradicting standard philosophy of science.
FIX: weaken to *defeasible/risky* refutability and relocate the asymmetry into the
normative governance of localization (the realist treats each blame-localization as a
further refutable conjecture, keeping truth-commitment at risk) — which needs a
demarcation criterion for ad hoc vs. legitimate auxiliary revision.

### GAP 3 — Primacy argument (Thm 1(i)) relies on the no-miracles premise (P0).
"The tool is reliable exactly insofar as the underlying description is approximately
true" IS the no-miracles argument, precisely what instrumentalists and Laudan's
pessimistic meta-induction reject. Caloric, phlogiston, optical ether, Ptolemaic
astronomy, Newtonian gravitation are reliable-within-range instruments with
now-false "underlying descriptions" — direct counterexamples to "reliable iff
approximately true." Since the problem asks for the PRIMARY interpretation, this gap
is load-bearing: it is what is meant to lift the verdict from "(C) is present and
irreducible" to "(C) is primary." FIX: defend the dependence premise via
selective/structural realism (Worrall, Psillos) and confront Laudan's list.

### GAP 4 — "Essential appraisal" is undefined; eliminative instrumentalism
(Def 2(ii)) is never engaged (P1).
The argument hinges entirely on what is "essential to (C)." It never engages option
(ii) of its own Definition 2: the ELIMINATIVE instrumentalist grants the truth/
refutation talk but holds it is dispensable surplus to be explained away. Refuting
that requires showing truth/refutation vocabulary is INDISPENSABLE (does explanatory
work no (A)-vocabulary can do), not merely used. Prima facie the instrumentalist can
reconstruct the "lost distinctions" (empirically-adequate-so-far vs.
empirically-refuted; predictively-retained vs. predictively-superseded), defeating
Thm 1(ii). FIX: turn the indispensability CLAIM into an ARGUMENT — e.g. transfer of a
model to a NEW untested domain requires believed truth and cannot be underwritten by
a past-convenience surrogate.

### Minor / non-gap.
- Thm 3's strength inherits Gaps 1–4: absent their repair, the verdict holds only
  relative to the stipulative Definitions 1 and 3 — add a scope caveat that it
  refutes *strong/eliminative* instrumentalism, not necessarily van Fraassen.
- §5 Prop. 6 is sound but inherits Gap 2: the object exposed to refutation is the
  *test-system* `T ∧ auxiliaries ∧ c`, not `T` alone. Minor rephrase.

Full background, difficulty assessment, and suggested repairs (re-target thesis;
holism-aware refutability; selective realism; indispensability-via-novel-domain) are
in the attached "Critic Analysis" document.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Revision addressing the four critic gaps

I rewrote `solution.tex` so that the verdict is established against the **strongest** instrumentalist positions rather than by stipulation. Summary of fixes, keyed to the critic's gaps:

**GAP 1 (begs question vs. constructive empiricism).** Definition 2 now distinguishes three grades of instrumentalism: weak/pragmatic (I_w), eliminative (I_e), and empirical-adequacy/constructive-empiricist (I_a). Lemma 1 is restated as an explicitly *conditional* structural fact about I_w's own purpose-indexed vocabulary (no longer a disguised restatement of the thesis). The false-dichotomy loophole is closed by a new **Lemma 2 (Empirical adequacy underdetermines, and so cannot replace, descriptive content)**, argued via (i) observable underdetermination of unobservable structure and (ii) the parasitism of EA(T) on the descriptive derivation — not by definition. Proposition 1 now refutes reduction against both I_w and I_a.

**GAP 2 (Duhem–Quine).** I dropped naive single-counterexample falsification. New Definition (test-system S = T ∧ A ∧ c) and Definition (risk-preserving vs. risk-dissolving/ad hoc localization). Lemma 5 → **defeasible, holistic refutability**: a clash establishes ¬S, holism is conceded, and the asymmetry is relocated to the *normative governance* of localization. Proposition 2 explicitly answers the Lakatos/Kuhn objection ("realists revise auxiliaries too"): the contrast is not *whether* one localizes but *under what norm* (content-increasing, independently testable repairs vs. convenience-restoration), a distinction inexpressible in (A). §5 now exposes the *test-system*, not T alone (the minor §5 fix).

**GAP 3 (no-miracles / pessimistic meta-induction).** I no longer assert "reliable iff approximately true." New **Lemma (Selective dependence)** defends a structural/selective-realist version (Worrall, Psillos) and confronts Laudan's list directly (Fresnel's equations, Newtonian inverse-square as GR limit, Carnot vs. caloric): working/structural content is conserved across theory change, idle posits discarded. The dependence premise (Prop. Asymmetric dependence) now rests only on the surviving structural content.

**GAP 4 (eliminative instrumentalism / "essential" undefined).** Definition 4 now defines **dispensability/indispensability** precisely (existence of a salva-inference surrogate). New **Lemma (Past-convenience surrogates do not project)** and **Theorem (Indispensability)** turn the claim into an argument: projection of a theory into an *untested domain* D1 ⊄ D0 is licensed by the descriptive appraisal P but by no track-record surrogate P* (which is silent about D1); hence I_e fails.

The main theorem now separates irreducibility (vs. I_w, I_a), indispensability (vs. I_e), and primacy (asymmetric dependence + preservation of three distinctions). **Remark (Scope)** adds the requested caveat: the result refutes the *primacy* claim shared by all three; it is compatible with the modest constructive-empiricist view that *belief* in unobservables is optional — the conceptual question of theories' *function* is what is settled.

Checks: all `\ref` resolve to existing `\label`s; all `\begin/\end` balanced. No LaTeX toolchain installed in workspace so compilation was skipped.

Marking resolved.
